steps.title: Step 1
steps.content: A percent increase of 676% means the mass grew by 676% of its original value. The new mass equals original plus increase: $k + 6.76k = 7.76k$.

steps.title: Step 2
steps.content: Equivalently, multiply by the growth factor: $k \times (1 + 676/100) = k \times 7.76 = 7.76k$.

steps.title: Step 3
steps.content:
• The matching choice: $7.76k$ — equals $k \times (1 + 6.76)$, correctly applying a 676% increase. A is correct.
  • $6.76k$ — omits the original mass; this represents only the increase, not the final mass. Incorrect.
  • $1.08k$ — corresponds to an 8% increase, not 676%. Incorrect.
  • $0.08k$ — represents an 8% decrease from the original, not related to a 676% increase. Incorrect.
